generatedSentence: Anya, a disillusioned wildlife illustrator, dwelled in a cramped residence, her knees often aching. The dominant paradigm, enforced by invisible pilots, sought to void any variety of thought. She would often monitor the official paragraphs, taking a mental note of their dubious accuracy, yet forced to assume their truth. In her pocket, a small triceratops trophy was her only realistic connection to wilder times. This active suppression of art and the suffering it caused began to jeopardize her passive existence; she could no longer nourish herself with the old ways. One night, a lucid dream caused her outlook to darken, a pike-like threat looming. She began to visualize why the city was cloaked in gloom, a brainless system enforced by unseen gatekeepers. Nobody questioned the weight of the headmaster's iron rule. A powerful kickback system ensured compliance, much like poison seeping into the oak-panelled embassy. Anya felt a sudden nostalgia for simpler times. She found an ancient transporter, chucked in an alley, needing only matches, a small onion-shaped device she could operate with one finger. With a flickering of light, the transporter engaged, allowing her to wander, whisked away from the ghetto. Her wallet and keys were still in her pocket, a stroke of luck. She arrived at a remote meadow, the sounds of wildlife – flamingos, racoons – filling the air, a vast variety from the city's mournful songs. She realized she was facing herself, her past failures, her exile, head-on. The ground, composed of greywackes, felt solid. Suddenly, blinking aliens emerged, their signaling beams cutting through the air, their motive unknown, a gruesome gathering of cosmic beings. She needed jujitsu skills now. Jacky, a millionaire and her former photography tutor, appeared, having rewired a powerful satellite dish in his office, linking longitudes across the planet. He explained how they could minimize the threat by using powerful sonic bullets, a vivisection of the air itself, against the aliens. She joined him joyfully, a hockey player's determination in her stride. Covering vast distances backpacking, they shared cider, their bond strengthening. On Easter, they made the newsroom with their keynote victory, proving that even a nobody can be the luckiest survivor. They had wiped the invasion, becoming the main hope.
panelSentences: Anya, a disillusioned wildlife illustrator, dwelled in a cramped residence, her knees often aching. The dominant paradigm, enforced by invisible pilots, sought to void any variety of thought. She would often monitor the official paragraphs, taking a mental note of their dubious accuracy, yet forced to assume their truth. In her pocket, a small triceratops trophy was her only realistic connection to wilder times. This active suppression of art and the suffering it caused began to jeopardize her passive existence; she could no longer nourish herself with the old ways.

One night, a lucid dream caused her outlook to darken, a pike-like threat looming. She began to visualize why the city was cloaked in gloom, a brainless system enforced by unseen gatekeepers. Nobody questioned the weight of the headmaster's iron rule. A powerful kickback system ensured compliance, much like poison seeping into the oak-panelled embassy. Anya felt a sudden nostalgia for simpler times. She found an ancient transporter, chucked in an alley, needing only matches, a small onion-shaped device she could operate with one finger.

With a flickering of light, the transporter engaged, allowing her to wander, whisked away from the ghetto. Her wallet and keys were still in her pocket, a stroke of luck. She arrived at a remote meadow, the sounds of wildlife – flamingos, racoons – filling the air, a vast variety from the city's mournful songs. She realized she was facing herself, her past failures, her exile, head-on. The ground, composed of greywackes, felt solid. Suddenly, blinking aliens emerged, their signaling beams cutting through the air, their motive unknown, a gruesome gathering of cosmic beings. She needed jujitsu skills now.

Jacky, a millionaire and her former photography tutor, appeared, having rewired a powerful satellite dish in his office, linking longitudes across the planet. He explained how they could minimize the threat by using powerful sonic bullets, a vivisection of the air itself, against the aliens. She joined him joyfully, a hockey player's determination in her stride. Covering vast distances backpacking, they shared cider, their bond strengthening. On Easter, they made the newsroom with their keynote victory, proving that even a nobody can be the luckiest survivor. They had wiped the invasion, becoming the main hope.
rawPanelSentences: Anya, a disillusioned wildlife illustrator, dwelled in a cramped residence, her knees often aching. The dominant paradigm, enforced by invisible pilots, sought to void any variety of thought. She would often monitor the official paragraphs, taking a mental note of their dubious accuracy, yet forced to assume their truth. In her pocket, a small triceratops trophy was her only realistic connection to wilder times. This active suppression of art and the suffering it caused began to jeopardize her passive existence; she could no longer nourish herself with the old ways.

One night, a lucid dream caused her outlook to darken, a pike-like threat looming. She began to visualize why the city was cloaked in gloom, a brainless system enforced by unseen gatekeepers. Nobody questioned the weight of the headmaster's iron rule. A powerful kickback system ensured compliance, much like poison seeping into the oak-panelled embassy. Anya felt a sudden nostalgia for simpler times. She found an ancient transporter, chucked in an alley, needing only matches, a small onion-shaped device she could operate with one finger.

With a flickering of light, the transporter engaged, allowing her to wander, whisked away from the ghetto. Her wallet and keys were still in her pocket, a stroke of luck. She arrived at a remote meadow, the sounds of wildlife – flamingos, racoons – filling the air, a vast variety from the city's mournful songs. She realized she was facing herself, her past failures, her exile, head-on. The ground, composed of greywackes, felt solid. Suddenly, blinking aliens emerged, their signaling beams cutting through the air, their motive unknown, a gruesome gathering of cosmic beings. She needed jujitsu skills now.

Jacky, a millionaire and her former photography tutor, appeared, having rewired a powerful satellite dish in his office, linking longitudes across the planet. He explained how they could minimize the threat by using powerful sonic bullets, a vivisection of the air itself, against the aliens. She joined him joyfully, a hockey player's determination in her stride. Covering vast distances backpacking, they shared cider, their bond strengthening. On Easter, they made the newsroom with their keynote victory, proving that even a nobody can be the luckiest survivor. They had wiped the invasion, becoming the main hope.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a determined woman with short cropped silver hair and a worn leather jacket, carrying a small, glowing backpack. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist sits hunched in a cramped, dark apartment, illuminated by the glow of a small monitor, a miniature triceratops figurine clutched in her hand. Camera is a wide shot, slightly above eye level. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist's finger hovering over a glowing, antique transporter device in a derelict, neon-lit alleyway, sparks flying around it. Camera is a close-up on her hands and the device. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist stands in a lush, alien meadow with glowing flora, turning to face a group of imposing, insectoid aliens with bright, pulsating eyes. Camera is a medium shot, low angle, emphasizing the aliens' height. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist and a male companion, both in dynamic action poses, fire glowing sonic weapons at the retreating alien ships, vibrant energy trails across a sunrise sky. Camera is a dynamic wide shot.

definitions.definition: The place where one lives; one's home.
A building used as a home.
The place where a corporation is established.
The state of living in a particular place or environment.
Accommodation for students at a university or college.
The place where anything rests permanently.
Subsidence, as of a sediment
That which falls to the bottom of liquors; sediment; also, refuse; residuum.
A base of Russian espionage operations within a foreign country.
A pattern, a way of doing something, especially a pattern of thought, a system of beliefs, a conceptual framework.
definitions.example: Thomas Kuhn's landmark “The Structure of Scientific Revolutions” got people talking about paradigm shifts, to the point the word itself now suggests an incomplete or biased perspective.

definitions.definition: An example serving as the model for such a pattern.
A set of all forms which contain a common element, especially the set of all inflectional forms of a word or a particular grammatical category.
definitions.example: The paradigm of "go" is "go, went, gone."

definitions.definition: A passage in text that is about a different subject from the preceding text, marked by commencing on a new line, the first line sometimes being indented.
A mark or note set in the margin to call attention to something in the text, such as a change of subject.
An offset of 16 bytes in Intel memory architectures.
To sort text into paragraphs.
A person who steers a ship, a helmsman.
A person who knows well the depths and currents of a harbor or coastal area, who is hired by a vessel to help navigate the harbor or coast.
A guide book for maritime navigation.
An instrument for detecting the compass error.
A pilot vehicle.
A person authorised to drive such a vehicle during an escort.
A guide or escort through an unknown or dangerous area.
Something serving as a test or trial.
definitions.example: We would like to run a pilot in your facility before rolling out the program citywide.

definitions.definition: A person who is in charge of the controls of an aircraft.
A sample episode of a proposed TV series produced to decide if it should be made or not. If approved, typically the first episode of an actual TV series.
A cowcatcher.
A pilot light.
One who flies a kite.
A short plug, sometimes made interchangeable, at the end of a counterbore to guide the tool.
To control (an aircraft or watercraft).
To guide (a vessel) through coastal waters.
To test or have a preliminary trial of (an idea, a new product, etc.)
To authenticate by means of belief; to surmise; to suppose to be true, especially without proof
definitions.example: We assume that, as her parents were dentists, she knows quite a bit about dentistry.

definitions.definition: To take on a position, duty or form
definitions.example: Mr. Jones will assume the position of a lifeguard until a proper replacement is found.

definitions.definition: To adopt a feigned quality or manner; to claim without right; to arrogate

definitions.definition: To receive, adopt (a person)
To adopt (an idea or cause)
The state of being accurate; being free from mistakes, this exemption arising from carefulness; exactness; correctness
definitions.example: The value of testimony depends on its accuracy.

definitions.definition: Exact conformity to truth, or to a rule or model; degree of conformity of a measure to a true or standard value.
(heading) A symbol or annotation.
(heading) A written or printed communication or commitment.
(heading) A sound.
Observation; notice; heed.
Reputation; distinction.
definitions.example: a poet of note

definitions.definition: Notification; information; intelligence.

To notice with care; to observe; to remark; to heed.

definitions.definition: To record in writing; to make a memorandum of.

definitions.definition: To denote; to designate.

definitions.definition: To annotate.
To set down in musical characters.
To record on the back of (a bill, draft, etc.) a refusal of acceptance, as the ground of a protest, which is done officially by a notary.
Women's underwear or nightclothes, now especially when lacy or designed to be seductive.
Linen goods collectively.
Common name of the extinct genus Triceratops; a herbivorous ceratopsid from the late Cretaceous.
Tropæum.
An object, usually in the form of a statuette, cup, or shield, awarded for success in a competition or to mark a special achievement.

definitions.definition: An object taken as a prize by a hunter or conqueror, especially one that is displayed.

definitions.definition: Any emblem of success; a status symbol.
definitions.example: His trophies included his second wife, his successful children, the third and fourth homes in Palm Beach and Malibu, his three yachts (for the Pacific, the Atlantic, and the Mediterranean), his jet, and his mistresses.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An object taken by a serial killer or rapist as a memento of the crime.

Animals, plants, and fungi, not normally domesticated, often to the exclusion of plants, fungi, fish, insects and other invertebrates, and microscopic plants and animals; hence:

(Australian rhyming slang) Wife.

definitions.definition: To place something over or upon, as to conceal or protect.

definitions.definition: To protect or control (a piece or square).

Noise without meaning; empty noise.

To cover in front, for ornament, protection, etc.; to put a facing upon.
definitions.example: a building faced with marble

definitions.definition: To line near the edge, especially with a different material.
definitions.example: to face the front of a coat, or the bottom of a dress

definitions.definition: To cover with better, or better appearing, material than the mass consists of, for purpose of deception, as the surface of a box of tea, a barrel of sugar, etc.

To make the surface of (anything) flat or smooth; to dress the face of (a stone, a casting, etc.); especially, in turning, to shape or smooth the flat surface of, as distinguished from the cylindrical surface.
(retail) To arrange the products in (a store) so that they are tidy and attractive.
definitions.example: In my first job, I learned how to operate a till and to face the store to high standards.

definitions.definition: The most external portion of exterior siding.
Fabric applied to a garment edge on the underside.
A powdered substance, such as charcoal or bituminous coal, applied to the face of a mould, or mixed with the sand that forms it, to give a fine smooth surface to the casting.
(in the plural) The collar and cuffs of a military coat, commonly of a different colour from the rest of the coat.
(usually in the plural) The movement of soldiers by turning on their heels to the right, left, or about.
Positioned so as to face (in a particular direction)
(of points and crossovers) Diverging in the direction of travel.
A colorful, conspicuous structure associated with angiosperms, frequently scented and attracting various insects, and which may or may not be used for sexual reproduction.
A reproductive structure in angiosperms (flowering plants), often conspicuously colourful and typically including sepals, petals, and either or both stamens and/or a pistil.
A plant that bears flowers, especially a plant that is small and lacks wood.
definitions.example: We transplanted the flowers to a larger pot.

definitions.definition: (usually with in) Of plants, a state of bearing blooms.
definitions.example: The dogwoods are in flower this week.

definitions.definition: (hypocoristic) The vulva, especially the labia majora.
The best examples or representatives of a group.
definitions.example: We selected the flower of the applicants.

definitions.definition: The best state of things; the prime.
definitions.example: She was in the flower of her life.

definitions.definition: Flour.
(in the plural) A substance in the form of a powder, especially when condensed from sublimation.
definitions.example: the flowers of sulphur

definitions.definition: A figure of speech; an ornament of style.
Ornamental type used chiefly for borders around pages, cards, etc.
(in the plural) Menstrual discharges.
To put forth blooms.
definitions.example: This plant flowers in June.

definitions.definition: To decorate with pictures of flowers.
To reach a state of full development or achievement.
To froth; to ferment gently, as new beer.
To come off as flowers by sublimation.
Something that flows, such as a river.
Krumping: an energetic style of hip-hop dance
An alcoholic, often sparkling (carbonated) beverage made from fermented apples; hard cider; apple cider

A non-alcoholic still beverage consisting of the juice of early-harvest apples, usually unfiltered and still containing pulp; apple cider; sweet cider (without pulp such a beverage is called apple juice).
definitions.example: She liked an aged cider. He liked a harder cider.

definitions.definition: A non-alcoholic carbonated beverage made from apples.
(in Korea) A non-alcoholic drink, normally carbonated; equivalent to soft drink.
A cup, glass or serving of any of these beverages.
In a joyful manner; joyously.
An estimate that is too low.
To perceive (someone or something) as having a lower value, quantity, worth, etc., than what he/she/it actually has.
A device or fixture used for urination, particularly:
Any oblong glass vessel shaped like the old alchemist's urinal.
A room or structure used for urination: a latrine; an outhouse; a lavatory.
To burn or shine unsteadily, or with a wavering light.
To keep going on and off; to appear and disappear for short moments; to flutter.
To flutter; to flap the wings without flying.
A short, uncertain burst.
definitions.example: Even after the acrimonious breakup, she still felt flickerings of love for him.

definitions.definition: Shining unsteadily or vary rapidly in brightness (referring to a light or a source of light)
State or condition of not meeting a desirable or intended objective, opposite of success.
An object, person or endeavour in a state of failure or incapable of success.
Termination of the ability of an item to perform its required function; breakdown.
Bankruptcy.
The state of being banished from one's home or country.
definitions.example: He lived in exile.

definitions.definition: Someone who is banished from their home or country.
definitions.example: She lived as an exile.

definitions.definition: To send into exile.
Eastern.
The office of a news organisation, especially that part of it where the journalists work and news stories are processed.
A room where newspapers and magazines are available for reading.
Angular distance measured west or east of the prime meridian.
Any imaginary line perpendicular to the equator and part of a great circle passing through the North Pole and South Pole.
Length.
Short for mainline.
To mainly play a specific character, or side, during a game.
definitions.example: He mains the same character as me in that game.

definitions.definition: Of a road: to convert into a main or primary road.
Of chief or leading importance; prime, principal.
Chief, most important, or principal in extent, size, or strength; consisting of the largest part.
definitions.example: main timbers  main branch of a river  main body of an army

definitions.definition: (of force, strength, etc.) Full, sheer, undivided.
Big; angry.
Belonging to or connected with the principal mast in a vessel.
Great in size or degree; important, powerful, strong, vast.
Exceedingly, extremely, greatly, mightily, very, very much.
(strictly) A person whose net worth is at or greater than one million units of the local currency, but less than two million.
A person whose net worth is at or greater than one million units of the local currency; a multimillionaire.
To make (something) as small or as insignificant as possible.
To remove (a window) from the main display area, collapsing it to an icon or caption.
definitions.example: I didn't close anything, but I minimized all the windows so I could see the desktop.

definitions.definition: To treat (someone) slightingly.
A musical composition with lyrics for voice or voices, performed by singing.
definitions.example: Thomas listened to his favorite song on the radio yesterday.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Any musical composition.
Poetical composition; poetry; verse.
The act or art of singing.
A melodious sound made by a bird, insect, whale or other animal.
definitions.example: I love hearing the song of canary birds.

definitions.definition: The distinctive sound that a male bird utters to attract a mate or to protect his territory; contrasts with call
A low price, especially one under the expected value; chiefly in for a song.
definitions.example: He bought that car for a song.

definitions.definition: An object of derision; a laughing stock.
A sailor.
English gin.

A method of self-defence established in Japan emphasizing “jū” (soft or gentle) “jutsu” (art or technique). Japanese martial art that utilizes a large variety of techniques in defense against an opponent.
Brazilian jiu-jitsu, a related art, derived from judo.
Precision maneuvering.
A wading bird of the family Phoenicopteridae.
A deep pink color tinged with orange, like that of a flamingo.
A nocturnal omnivore native to North America, typically with a mixture of gray, brown, and black fur, a mask-like marking around the eyes and a striped tail; Procyon lotor.
Any mammal of the genus Procyon.
Any mammal of the subfamily Procyoninae, a procyonine.
Any mammal of the family Procyonidae, a procyonid.
A hard dark sandstone with poorly sorted angular grains of quartz, feldspar, and small rock fragments in a compact, clay-fine matrix.
